(By United Press Leased Wire)
VTYAsHlNGToN, Feb. 1s.-reIe.
wv phone bells In the executive


W offices and the senate com.
mittee rooms today tinkled the
knell of large portion of President
Taft's legislation.


Conferences at the white house
and conferences at the capitol dur.
Ing the past week, eliminating In a
conference between the president
and Senator Aldrich this afternoon,
have resulted In the decision that
four Of the Taft projects will be-
come laws at the present session of
congress. ..',


Senator Aldrieh and his lieuten-
ants, recognizing that the country
demands some constructive legisla-
tion, have agreed to rush the pas
sage of the following measures,
sidestepping the others:


The interstate commerce amend
ment bill, the postal savings bank
bill, the anti-injunction bill, and the
Arizona-New Mexico statehood bill.


POSTAL SAVINGS FIGHT.


President Taft and the leaders
are trying to gather a majority for
the postal savings bank bill in the
senate. They have abandoned the
Root amendment providing for the
Investment Of postal savings funds
In government bonds in order to win
certain opponents of the measure
who objected only to the Root pro-
pOsals.


Senator Smoot has been cajoied
and threatened to induce him to
abandon his scheme of forcing the
deposit OF postal savings funds in
local state and national banks.


The president is willing to accept
the anti-injunction measure intro
duced In the house by Representa-
tive Moon, as it differs from the
one framed by the administration
only in that 1t Is more specific in
limiting the use of the Injunction.
The statehood bill for Arizona and
New Mexico, to which President
Taft clings tenaciously, has already
passed the house and Is now in
the senate committee on territories.
Although 1t Is expected to add to
the democratic strength in both
houses rhe president insists that it
be passed, to carry out the pledge
Of he platform of 1908.


Senator Beveridge's Alaskan
civil government bill was handled
so-roughly In the senate that 1t has
paen permanently retired.


The original railroad hill amend
ing Il e interstate commerce law he's
been withdrawn and Representative
Towns, nd of the house committee
on il terstate commerce has intro
ducef revised edition. Little diff.
ficulty is expected in getting This
measure through.


CONSERVATION MEASURES


LOST.


OF the conservation measures
only the bill authorizing the presi-
dent to withdraw from entry pend
ing the action of congress public
lands of any character and the gen.
eral classification measures are
ma being pushed with any vigor
The coal and oil lands and the
water power site conservation bills
have been lost in the shuttle.


AS for the federal incorporation
bill. 1t has been passed up for con-
sideration next winter.
